summary: Merged decomp progress for the gm_1A4C game-mode slice, centered on Tyfall/registry-style display setup. The PR replaced several stubs with C implementations, exposed shared gmregtyfall scene globals through headers, and improved matching enough for the bot to report +1112 matched bytes, including new 100% matches for gm_801A8D54 and fn_801A7FB4.
what_changed: Implemented formerly stubbed gm_1A4C routines: gm_801A7B00, gm_801A85E4, gm_801A8D54, gm_801A9094, and gm_801A9630.

gm_801A7B00 now builds light, camera, main JObj, and character-display GObjs; loads joints and animations; applies character translate/rotation/scale data through fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSet* wrappers; then constrains the display JObj to a walked JObj-tree target with lb_8000C1C0 and lb_8000C290.
fn_801A7FB4 was refactored to call two inline helpers that count enabled entries with un_803048C0(gm_801A659C(i)); the automated report says this moved fn_801A7FB4 from 99.14% to 100%.
gm_801A85E4 now computes JObj placement using range-specific angle formulas, HSD_Randf, cosf, sinf, and hard-coded fallback positions for later indices.
Added gm_80480A00 and gm_80480AD0 static arrays in gm_1A4C.c for character-display GObjs and random sort/order state.

gm_801A8D54 now builds a 0x1A-entry candidate list using sentinel 0x1A, filters enabled characters, handles CKIND_ZELDA and CKIND_SEAK cases separately for indices 0x12/0x13, excludes the current character in the general case, and sorts candidates by gm_80480AD0.

gm_801A9094 now creates display stand GObjs from TyDspEntry data, resolves joints and material animations from gm_804D679C with HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, loads ToyDspStand_Top_joint, attaches/animates child JOBJs, and positions roots through gm_801A85E4.

gm_801A9630 now initializes display state, assigns random ordering values with HSD_Randi, creates fog/light/camera/background JObjs, runs character display setup through gm_801A9094 and gm_801A4B90, then creates and constrains the selected-character JObj.
gm_1A4C.h replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete signatures for gm_801A7B00, gm_801A85E4, gm_801A8D54, gm_801A9094, and gm_801A9630.

gmregtyfall.c changed gm_804D6798, gm_804D679C, gm_804D67A0, gm_804D67A4, gm_804D67A8, and gm_804D67AC from static file-local globals to exported globals, and gmregtyfall.h added matching extern declarations plus a sc forward include.
smart_moves: Promoted the gmregtyfall scene/asset globals to header-visible externs instead of duplicating unknown declarations in gm_1A4C, making the split-file dependency explicit.
Converted unknown public prototypes to typed signatures once the implementations revealed void returns and HSD_JObj/s32 parameters.
Preserved exact HSD setup ordering: GObj_Create, HSD_GObjObject_80390A70, GX link setup, animation add/request/interpret, then HSD_GObj_SetupProc.
Used sentinel-filled fixed-size arrays and address-named BSS symbols to model the original character list and randomized ordering state.
Kept two separate inline count helpers for fn_801A7FB4 even though they are logically identical, which is supported by the bot reporting a full match for that function.
Used archive public-address lookups for display assets rather than hardcoding concrete asset structures, matching how this code obtains per-character display joints and matanims.
decomp_lessons: For game-mode scene setup code, matching often depends on preserving object creation order, gxlink callback choice, gxlink priority values such as 0x801, and proc priorities such as 0x17.
If a later split unit needs assets loaded by an earlier unit, expose the owning unit's globals through a real header with forward declarations rather than leaving them static.
Use concrete headers and types as soon as evidence supports them; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with HSD_JObj*, s32*, and void signatures improves both call-site readability and future matching work.

Artificial inline wrappers can be justified when they recover instruction layout; this PR's duplicate fn_801A7FB4_inline helpers are a reusable pattern for small repeated loops that must codegen differently at distinct call sites.
Keep decompiler-looking control flow when needed for matching, including ternary bool normalization, empty branches, odd loop initializers, and verbose compare expressions.
Character roster/list routines in this area use 0x1A as both loop bound and sentinel, and special-case Zelda/Sheik character kinds with CKIND_ZELDA and CKIND_SEAK.
naming_conventions: Unidentified game-mode functions keep address-based names such as gm_801A7B00, gm_801A85E4, gm_801A8D54, gm_801A9094, and gm_801A9630.
Local callback-like functions retain fn_ address names such as fn_801A7FB4, fn_801A7A44, fn_801A7A68, and fn_801A7A8C.
Globals remain address-based, for example gm_804D6798, gm_804D679C, gm_804D67A0, gm_804D67A4, gm_804D67A8, gm_804D67AC, gm_80480A00, and gm_80480AD0.
Header comments preserve symbol addresses next to declarations, e.g. /* 1A7B00 */ and /* 4D6798 */.
Artificial helpers use explicit _inline suffixes, e.g. fn_801A7FB4_inline, fn_801A7FB4_inline2, and gm_801A8114_inline.
The fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSet* prefix marks wrapper setter calls used in this Tyfall matching context instead of plain HSD_JObjSet* calls.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK values in newly decompiled routines, including 0x30, 0x20, 0x68, 0x88, and 8, to control stack layout.
Split identical count loops into two inline functions for fn_801A7FB4; the bot reported fn_801A7FB4 reached 100%.
Preserved odd source constructs such as un_803048C0(...) ? true : false, for-loops initialized with i = count, and an empty if (jobj == NULL) branch.
Used a verbose conditional expression in the gm_801A8D54 sort comparison instead of simplifying it, likely to preserve codegen shape.
Added a one-call gm_801A8114_inline wrapper before gm_801A9630's selected-character setup, another inline/codegen steering tactic.
Retained explicit repeated HSD_JObjGetChild calls to walk fixed-depth JObj trees before applying lb_8000C1C0 and lb_8000C290 constraints.
Included <MSL/math.h> and used cosf/sinf with degree-to-radian constant 0.017453292f for gm_801A85E4 placement formulas.
review_feedback: No PR body, human reviews, or review comments were present in the slice, so reviewer intent is not available.
The decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 64.46% (+0.03%, +1112 bytes), with 2 new matches: gm_801A8D54 at 100% (+832 bytes) and fn_801A7FB4 at 100% (+3 bytes).

The same bot report listed partial improvements for gm_801A85E4 at 99.60%, gm_801A9630 at 96.16%, gm_801A7B00 at 97.59%, gm_801A9094 at 96.65%, and data-section improvements in .bss, .sdata2, .sdata, and .sbss.
follow_up_queries: gm_801A85E4 99.60 cosf sinf HSD_Randf placement matching
gm_801A9630 96.16 fog light camera GObj gxlink_prios 0x801
gm_801A8D54 sentinel 0x1A CKIND_ZELDA CKIND_SEAK gm_80480AD0 sort
